title: vitex trifolia var. heterophylla
intro: Vitex trifolia var. heterophylla, also known as the Chinese chastetree or Chinese vitex, is a plant species within the Lamiaceae family, native to Asia and known for its medicinal and health benefits. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Vitex trifolia var. heterophylla include its potential to alleviate menstrual cramps, menopausal symptoms, and anxiety, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect against chronic diseases. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support women's health, cardiovascular well-being, and immune system function.
